title: A Look At Twist Bioscience (TWST) Valuation After Strong Results Guidance Raise And Analyst Optimism
text: Twist Bioscience has returned to investor focus following the release of strong quarterly results that demonstrated year-over-year revenue growth alongside an elevated full-year revenue forecast. This positive momentum arrives alongside renewed analyst enthusiasm, though recent profit-taking has contributed to market volatility. The company's recent performance reflects broader strength, with shares gaining 16.40% over the last 30 days and 55.54% year-to-date. However, longer-term returns have been mixed—while the stock achieved approximately 3.4x gains over three years, it experienced a 60.54% decline over five years, suggesting accelerating momentum around the company's growth narrative. The stock closed at $50.38 against a calculated fair value of approximately $35.63, indicating a 41.4% overvaluation according to Simply Wall St's analysis model. The assessment attributes this premium to expectations regarding the company's silicon-based manufacturing advantages. On a price-to-sales basis, the stock trades at 7.9x compared to 11x for the broader US biotech sector and 16.8x for direct competitors. The analysis highlights structural strengths including cost, speed, and scale advantages from the company's proprietary manufacturing process. However, potential vulnerabilities include continuing adjusted EBITDA losses and concentration risk from a limited customer base focused on next-generation sequencing applications.
